lyrics:
Two clocks on the wall
the morning catches them
still swinging as one

Moon pulls the sea
the small bone in the ear
answers, still

A bow across one string
beside it, untouched
another sings

Fireflies over the hill
each alone
then
all at once

Two things kept apart
given long enough
the gap begins to close

A bridge full of strangers
falling into step
the swaying deepens

The children learn the room
its rhythm, its hush
before they learn their names

I watched you sleep
and my breath
found the rise and fall of yours

Two things kept apart
given long enough
the gap begins to close

The planet turns
locked to its star
one face turned always to the sun

Two things kept apart
given long enough
the gap begins to close

impression.text: This track establishes an intimate, soulful atmosphere with a texture of warm electric piano, crisp, live-sounding drums, and a prominent, melodic fretless bass. The energy is cool and laid-back, driven by a relaxed R&B groove that feels both tight and spacious. The production quality is high, creating a clean and open soundscape where the breathy male vocal sits comfortably atop the well-balanced instrumental arrangement. A defining quality is the conversational interplay between the articulate bass lines and the emotive vocal melody, creating a sophisticated and flowing musical dialogue.

review.review_text: Rose builds 'Entraining' around one of physics' quieter miracles: the way two oscillating things, left close enough, eventually fall into sync. In 3/4 time at a patient 117 BPM, the song itself does exactly that, a fretless bass and breathy vocal circling each other until they settle into the same groove. The lyric earns its central image steadily, moving from clocks and tides to a bow making an untouched string vibrate in sympathy, before landing where it always meant to: 'I watched you sleep / and my breath / found the rise and fall of yours.' That line does the work quietly, the way the best lines do. A standout cut.
anchors_used: fretless bass texture from audio impression
quoted lyric: 'I watched you sleep / and my breath / found the rise and fall of yours'
central metaphor of entrainment traced through song structure
